relatedness_reasoning: Both files involve credit assessment and customer evaluation; inbound file contains lending decision override with credit score and risk details, outbound file is a credit score result sent back to risk_and_compliance. The systems form a bidirectional pipeline where credit processes override requests and returns assessment results. Key overlaps include customer_id, credit score, debt-to-income ratio, and compliance flags.

mappings.reasoning: No equivalent identifier in inbound; likely system-generated unique result identifier.
Both are ISO 8601 timestamps; outbound timestamp likely derived from or correlated with inbound message timestamp.
Inbound destination is 'credit'; outbound source_department is 'credit', indicating the credit system is now the source of the result.
Inbound source is 'risk_and_compliance'; outbound destination_department is 'risk_and_compliance', indicating the result flows back to the originating system.
Exact semantic match; customer identifier from inbound payload mapped to outbound customer object.
Inbound schema contains no customer name field; this information is not available in the override message.
Inbound schema contains no date of birth field; this demographic information is not provided in the override message.
Inbound schema contains no account number or IBAN field; banking details are not included in the override message.
Exact semantic match; credit score integer from inbound risk assessment details mapped to outbound score field.
Inbound schema does not specify credit score range boundaries; these are likely system defaults or configuration values.
Inbound schema does not specify credit score range boundaries; these are likely system defaults or configuration values.
Inbound schema provides only numeric credit score, not categorical rating; rating is likely derived from score via lookup table not present in inbound data.
Inbound approval date is the closest temporal reference to when the credit assessment was performed; may represent assessment date.
Inbound schema contains loan amount but not total credit limit; these are distinct credit concepts not present in override message.
Inbound schema does not contain outstanding balance or debt information; this is not provided in the override message.
Inbound schema does not provide credit utilization data; this metric is not available in the override message.
Inbound schema does not contain payment history score; only years of income history is provided, which is a different concept.
Inbound schema does not track delinquency counts; this risk metric is not included in the override message.
Inbound risk assessment details (debt-to-income, loan-to-value, mitigating factors) inform default risk, but no explicit risk level enum is provided.
Inbound override decision is 'APPROVED'; outbound recommended action is 'Approve', representing the same approval recommendation.
Inbound schema does not explicitly assess fraud risk; compliance flags cover AML and sanctions but not fraud.
Inbound compliance flags object (amlCheckCompleted, sanctionsCheckCompleted, creditPolicyWaiverRequired) maps to outbound compliance_flags array.
Inbound schema does not summarize regulatory concerns; only specific compliance checks are documented.
Inbound approval level indicates the override was reviewed and approved; outbound review_status 'Completed' reflects this, though not a direct match.
Inbound approvedBy identifier maps to outbound reviewed_by; both represent the person/system that performed the assessment review.
Inbound schema does not provide cryptographic signatures or audit signatures; this is a security artifact not present in the override message.
